subject: Re: Particle recentering from Relion to Warp
body:
Hi Warpers,

Thank you for the enlightening discussion about this topic. I am having a related problem where I have subtomogram averages of a particle and am decreasing the binning as I refine and discard junk. Going from 8x to 4x binning didn't seem to be a problem (reconstructing an average from 10,000 particles gave a reasonable shape although weak signal, but a round of refinement hugely improved the signal), but going from 4x to 2x is giving problems. If I try to reconstruct a random average at this binning there is a huge ball of noise unless I apply a low pass filter, and refinement in Relion crashes during a ~300 hour iteration and the halfmap from the iteration before looks like nothing.

I am wondering if during the subtomogram extraction in Warp there is something that I am missing. I take the output star file from refinement in Relion and modify it for Warp (remove most of the columns except CoordinateX/Y/Z, AngleRot/Tilt/Psi, MicrographName and OriginX/Y/Z (with the last numbers untouched). I am just trying manually subtracting the offset (after pixel size correction) from the CoordinateX/Y/Z and zero'ing it, but I assume Warp should do that by default?

Is this likely to be the issue or am I missing something else at this unbinning step? Thanks a lot for this helpful community.

Many thanks,
Tom
